questionHtml:
Iron (II) sulfide reacts with hydrochloric acid to form hydrogen sulfide, H2S.

In aqueous solution, hydrogen sulfide acts as an acid.

State the formula of its conjugate base.

meta.title: State the formula of its conjugate base.
breadcrumbLabels: Reactivity 3. What are the mechanisms of chemical change?
Reactivity 3.1.2—A pair of species differing by a single proton is called a conjugate acid–base pair. Deduce the formula of the conjugate acid or base of any Br.nsted–Lowry base or acid.
